title: scout-report Decision Trace goal_hash must be machine-stamped, never LLM-transcribed — a one-hex-digit typo aborted a lane (lane-scope coherence guard, verify-run 2026-07-19)
why: Live 2-lane verify run: lane merge-rung2-wire-ship-recovery aborted at triage with 'lane-scope goal-hash mismatch' — scout-report Decision Trace goal_hash …c05356e vs pinned lane-scope.json …c05376e. Exactly ONE hex digit differs: the scout LLM hand-copied the 64-char hash into its report and typo'd it. The guard did its job (refused an incoherent lane identity, task-level abort, loop continued), but the whole failure class is manufactured: a value the orchestrator MINTED and PINNED is round-tripped through an LLM's transcription and then string-compared against itself.
how_to_apply: Single-source-with-projection: the runner (or the scout deliverable contract post-processor) should stamp the canonical goal_hash into the scout-report Decision Trace mechanically — e.g. template substitution at prompt-composition or a post-write normalization that replaces the Decision Trace goal_hash line with the pinned lane-scope value + flags a WARN if the LLM-written value differed. The guard's comparison then verifies pipeline plumbing, not LLM typing accuracy. Zero prompt changes needed for agents beyond removing the hand-copy instruction.
evidence: loop-verify2 run 2026-07-19: 'evolve cycle run: lane-scope goal-hash mismatch: scout-report Decision Trace goal_hash "…c05356e" != pinned lane-scope.json goal_hash "…c05376e" — triage must not run on an incoherent lane identity'
